pubmed_links: [Douglas et al. (2018) De novo missense variants in MEIS2 recapitulate the microdeletion phenotype of cardiac and palate abnormalities, developmental delay, intellectual disability and dysmorphic features](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30055086/); [Louw et al. (2019) Heterozygous loss-of-function variants of MEIS2 cause a triad of palatal defects, congenital heart defects, and intellectual disability](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30291340/); [Fabretto et al. (2018) MEIS2 gene is responsible for intellectual disability, cardiac defects and a distinct facial phenotype](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30735726/)
summary: Heterozygous loss-of-function variants and 15q14 microdeletions affecting MEIS2 cause a syndromic neurodevelopmental disorder featuring intellectual disability, cleft palate, congenital heart defects, and autism spectrum disorder in a subset of patients; MEIS2 haploinsufficiency produces ASD-like behavioral abnormalities in animal models. (well-established)
